calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Continued To Mar-06-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena And For Expenses Of The Motion
Off Calendar Dec-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court indicates it received a message that the matter is resolved prior to the hearing but there is no settlement on file at this time. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALPHA BUIE's Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena And For Expenses Of The Motion.

The tentative ruling is confirmed subject to further information of a settlement, in which the matter may be taken off-calendar.

The Court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for the recommendation in the report of Judge Pro Tem James Fleming. (See attached report). The subpoena to Wells Fargo Bank shall be enforced as to transactions with alleged former tenant Ronald Brooks, including payments or loans to or from Mr. Brooks, from May 1, 2015 to the present. The subpoena shall further be enforced to the extent of the monthly balances of the accounts in question for the period of May 16, 2016 to the present. Except as so ordered, the subpoena shall be quashed without prejudice as an overbroad invasion of plaintiff's financial privacy without a showing of elements required by law. The Court further finds that the remaining part of the subpoena is not relevant and not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The Court denies the request for sanctions.

Counsel for Plaintiff Alpha Blue must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Andrew Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR #5525, vgross@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AC)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, And To Compel Production Of Documents, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration
Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ted A. Smith, Esq. of Berger Kahn, Tel. 415.891.3321, for defendant and moving party;
Christine Chorney, Esq. of Casalina & Disston, Tel. 510.835.8110 for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court upheld the Judge Pro Tem's recommendation. DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LTD'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, And To Compel Production Of Documents is DENIED for reasons stated on the record. The prevailing party is to submit a proposed order that tracks the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, Tel. 415.482.9030.
